evidence: The probe-and-bars covariation is the single load-bearing system; the amber probe and the leading lilac bar draw the eye while the still periwinkle candidates support.
One integrated widget of probe, candidate arrows, and score bars is presented; no more than four distinct concepts appear together.
c1..c4 sit at their arrow tips and above their bars, 'probe' sits on the amber arrow, and no legend or key appears anywhere.
The only prose is the short instruction 'Turn the probe. Each bar tracks how closely it points at that candidate,' which annotates the visible widget below it.
Bar labels and mono readouts (0.34, 1.00, -1.00, etc.) are clearly readable at all three widths, and the in-circle 'probe 99°' and tip labels remain legible even at 375px.
The circle, all arrows, bars, and readouts sit entirely within each frame with generous margins, including the down-left probe in anti-aligned.
The 'probe' text touches only its own amber arrow (its referent), and candidate labels, bars, and readouts do not overlap unrelated content.
Amber marks the attention probe, periwinkle the observed candidate arrows, lilac the parameter score bars, and cream the labels — all consistent with Night School token semantics.
Every element carries content; the guide circle marks the unit direction space and there are no mascots or ornaments.

All four states deliver the promised moments: aligned c1=1.00 peak, orthogonal c3=1.00 with c1=0.00, anti-aligned c1=-1.00 below baseline, and initial near-vertical with the two central bars leading (probe reads 99° rather than exactly 90°, a minor label deviation).
Each bar is tied to its candidate by a shared c1..c4 label, so the score row reads as belonging to the arrows despite sitting below the circle.
Not applicable: these are independent driven states rather than a morph sequence to judge for continuity.
The composition has clear margins and a top-to-bottom reading order from instruction to circle to score bars, with ample negative space.
